title: Dense German Dark Chocolate Tart
description: A dense, rich, and indulgent German-inspired dessert featuring dark chocolate, infused-oil, and a buttery pastry crust. Perfect for a special occasion or romantic date-night.

ingredients.name: unsalted butter
ingredients.notes: chilled and cut into small pieces

ingredients.name: infused oil (such as hazelnut or almond)
ingredients.notes: optional, but recommended for added depth

instructions: Preheat the oven to 350F. Line a 9-inch tart pan with parchment paper.

In a medium bowl, whisk together the flour, confectioners' sugar, and salt. Add the cold butter and use a pastry blender or your fingers to work it into the flour mixture until it resembles coarse crumbs.
Press the mixture into the prepared tart pan, leaving a 1-inch border around the edges. Chill in the freezer for 15 minutes.
Bake the crust for 20-25 minutes, or until lightly golden.
In a separate bowl, melt the chocolate chips in the microwave in 30-second increments, stirring between each interval until smooth.
Spread the melted chocolate over the baked tart crust, leaving a 1-inch border around the edges.
If using infused oil, drizzle it over the chocolate in a zig-zag pattern.
Let the tart cool completely in the pan before slicing and serving.
